actionUnits.condition: Player (fileID 1121179982), a child of Island (fileID 1300569560), has Rigidbody (present per Has_Rigidbody:true) and BoxCollider (id 1121179988, enabled). It has MonoBehaviour id 1121179983 referencing script GUID 31b5de8685012e642b97f59bb31caf8d, which matches the provided SpacialMovement class. Scene settings map directly to script fields: moveSpeed=1, rotationSpeed=2, limitX(490,560), limitY(3.5,15), limitZ(515,570), delayToStartMovement=2. No UnityEvent m_Calls are present to invoke directly, and SpacialMovement exposes no public parameterless methods; Start() and Update() are engine-driven and startMovement() is private and invoked by Invoke after delay. This Trigger holds for >2s so Start() runs, Invoke("startMovement", 2f) fires, allowMovement becomes true, and Update() executes on several frames. To achieve full code coverage (WASD/mouse/Space/LeftControl/ESC branches and clamp edges), we need either input simulation hooks or a public API to drive movement/rotation.

actionUnits.condition: Moon (fileID 227156923), child of Island (fileID 1300569560), has MonoBehaviour id 227156925 with m_Script guid 243873248f8e3f6468b009a9e2d86557, which matches the provided MoonController class. The serialized scene settings map to the script: field 'player' is assigned to Transform (fileID 1121179986) of Player. Player has Rigidbody/BoxCollider, so using a Trigger action on Player is valid. MoonController.Start() is empty; Update() runs every frame and computes Quaternion.LookRotation(player.position - transform.position) then assigns transform.rotation. Holding this Trigger for 1.0s allows multiple Update() cycles to execute and cover the script’s behavior. No UnityEvent m_Calls or public parameterless methods are exposed for manual invocation.
